items.headline: US-Gericht verlängert Stopp für Warner-Übernahme durch Paramount
items.summary: Die milliardenschwere Übernahme von Warner Bros. Discovery durch Paramount verzögert sich weiter. Ein US-Gericht hat den vorläufigen Stopp bis Mitte August verlängert und prüft eine Klage mehrerer Bundesstaaten.

items.headline: Warken als Kanzleramtschefin, Linnemann als Gesundheitsminister bestätigt
items.summary: Kanzler Merz hat bestätigt, dass Nina Warken auf den Posten der Kanzleramtsministerin wechselt. Der bisherige CDU-Generalsekretär Carsten Linnemann übernimmt das Gesundheitsressort. Weitere Veränderungen sollen folgen.

items.headline: Gewinn bricht erneut ein - VW senkt Umsatzausblick
items.summary: Volkswagen hat im zweiten Quartal deutlich weniger verdient als ein Jahr zuvor. Zugleich rechnet der Autobauer für das Gesamtjahr mit niedrigeren Erlösen als bisher. Belastend wirkt unter anderem das schwache China-Geschäft.

items.headline: Merz baut Kabinett um: Verkehrsminister Schnieder muss offenbar gehen
items.summary: Bundeskanzler Merz wird nach Informationen des ARD-Hauptstadtstudios auch das Verkehrsministerium neu besetzen. Ressortchef Schnieder muss offenbar gehen. Für seine Nachfolge ist der CDU-Politiker Fritz Güntzler im Gespräch. Eine Bestätigung gibt es bislang nicht.

items.headline: Ankündigung der US-Regierung: Neue Zölle gegen 60 Handelspartner
items.summary: Die US-Regierung hat überraschend neue Einfuhrabgaben für 60 Handelspartner angekündigt. Bei diesen Zöllen handelt es sich um Strafen wegen angeblich unzureichender Maßnahmen gegen Zwangsarbeit. Von Carsten Kühntopp.

items.headline: US-Präsident Trump verhängt neue Zölle für 60 Länder - was jetzt gilt
items.summary: Die einen Zölle laufen aus, die nächsten sind bereits in den Startlöchern: US-Präsident Trump hat neue Zölle von bis zu 12,5 Prozent auf Importe aus 60 Ländern verhängt. Worauf die Maßnahmen beruhen und was sie bedeuten.

items.headline: Personalumbau der Union: Merz will Wechsel im Kabinett bekanntmachen
items.summary: Kanzleramtschef Frei soll Nachfolger von Unionsfraktionschef Spahn werden. Das hat Kanzler Merz bereits öffentlich gemacht. Am Vormittag wird er verkünden, wie das Stühlerücken in seinem Kabinett weitergeht.

items.headline: So lief die Debatte um die Guterres-Nachfolge
items.summary: Wer folgt auf UN-Generalsekretär Guterres? Sechs Bewerberinnen und Bewerber stellten sich in einer öffentlichen Debatte vor. Was sie einte: der Wunsch nach starken Vereinten Nationen. Von Martin Ganslmeier.

items.headline: Adobe-Chrome-Erweiterung ermöglichte Datenklau
items.summary: In der Chrome-Erweiterung Adobe Acrobat mit 312 Millionen Nutzern klaffte eine Schwachstelle. Dadurch konnten Angreifer Daten stehlen.

items.headline: Günstiger Standort nötig: Partielle Sonnenfinsternis am Abend des 12. Augusts
items.summary: Die untergehende Sonne wird am 12. August auch über dem deutschsprachigen Raum partiell vom Mond verdeckt. Für die Beobachtung muss nicht nur das Wetter passen.

items.headline: heise+ | Neuer Sensorchip der ETH Zürich macht Deepfakes unmöglich
items.summary: Forscher der ETH Zürich haben ein Sensorsystem entwickelt, das Bild- und Tondaten direkt im Chip kryptografisch signiert, um Manipulationen zu verhindern.

items.headline: Russische Angreifer missbrauchen Zero-Click-Lücke in Zimbra
items.summary: Russische Angreifer attackieren seit Juli 2025 westliche Regierungen und Einrichtungen durch eine Zero-Click-Lücke in Zimbra.

items.headline: „Russlands Amazon“: Ukraine greift immer mehr Logistikzentren von Wildberries an
items.summary: Die Ukraine hat in den vergangenen Tagen immer wieder Lager von Wildberries angegriffen, Russlands größtem Onlinehändler. Die würden auch fürs Militär genutzt.

items.headline: Intels 11 Milliarden US-Dollar Nettoverlust existieren nur auf dem Papier
items.summary: Die Börse honoriert ein starkes Intel-Quartal. Der ausgewiesene milliardenschwere Verlust existiert ausschließlich in den Büchern.

items.headline: Chatkontrolle: EU-Rat stimmt für die Verlängerung
items.summary: Nach ihrem parlamentarischen Coup haben die EU-Länder wie erwartet der erneuten Verlängerung einer Ausnahmeregelung für die Chatkontrolle zugestimmt.

items.headline: Identifikationspflicht: Dobrindt will Informationsfreiheit faktisch stoppen
items.summary: Mit Klarnamenzwang und der Abschaffung der Aufsichtsbehörde will das Innenministerium Transparenzrechte von Bürgern, Presse und Politik massiv beschneiden.

items.headline: Plattformwechsel: Apple Maps zieht fest in Fords E-Autos ein
items.summary: Ford baut Apple Maps direkt in seine kommende Elektroauto-Plattform ein. Den Anfang macht 2027 ein neuer Pick-up für rund 30.000 Dollar. (Ford, Elektroauto)

items.headline: Innere Sicherheit: Innenministerium zielt auf Kern der Informationsfreiheit
items.summary: Das Innenministerium plant offenbar weit mehr als eine Reform des Informationsfreiheitsgesetzes. Ein internes Papier zeigt, wohin die Reise geht. (BMI, Politik)

items.headline: Elektrifizierung vorantreiben: Einfachere Energiewende ohne Wasserstoff
items.summary: Werden Wärmeerzeugung und Transport elektrifiziert, gibt es kaum noch Verwendung für Wasserstoff. Ein ganzes Pipelinenetz könnte man sich sparen. (Wasserstoff, Brennstoffzelle)

items.headline: Tesla: Cybercab, Semi und Megapack verschieben sich
items.summary: Tesla schiebt die Serienproduktion seiner wichtigsten Zukunftsprojekte weiter nach hinten. Gleichzeitig wachsen die Ausgaben und der Gewinn schrumpft. (Tesla, KI)

items.headline: Messaging-App: Whatsapp erlaubt die eigenständige Registrierung auf dem iPad
items.summary: Whatsapp ermöglicht jetzt die Registrierung auf dem iPad ohne Smartphone-Koppelung. Zudem folgen Updates für Autos, PDFs und Musik-Status. (Whatsapp, Instant Messenger)

items.headline: Smart #5 Brabus im Test: Familienkutschen-Sleeper mit unerwartet viel Wumms und Gebimmel
items.summary: Die Brabus-Version von Smarts #5 mit 646 PS beschleunigt rasant und ist ein großartiges Langstreckenauto. Die Software nervt allerdings oft. Ein Praxistest von Tobias Költzsch (Elektroauto, Test)

items.headline: Windows 11: Microsoft testet Ausschalter für die Copilot-Taste
items.summary: Microsoft könnte die umstrittene Copilot-Taste bald komplett abschaltbar machen. In Vorschauversionen taucht dafür die Einstellung "Do nothing" auf. (Tastatur, Microsoft)

items.headline: Datenpanne: OpenAI-Crawler greift Kundendaten von Universa ab
items.summary: Durch eine Fehlkonfiguration sind Kundendaten der Universa-Versicherungen zeitweise offen im Netz gestanden. Ein Crawler von OpenAI hat die Gelegenheit genutzt. (Datenleck, KI)

items.headline: US hits dozens of trading partners with new wave of tariffs
items.summary: Imposed citing forced labour concerns, the levies replace a temporary global duty brought in after the US Supreme Court struck down tariffs in February.

items.headline: India's 'cockroach' protesters and government to hold talks as stalemate continues
items.summary: The meeting comes hours after activist Sonam Wangchuk announced he was ending his 26-day hunger strike.

items.headline: Families still search for bodies a month after Venezuela earthquakes
items.summary: Officials say the death toll has surpassed 5,300, though estimates of the number still missing could be as high as 50,000.

items.headline: Houthi attacks raise fears of wider Middle East conflict and more global economic damage
items.summary: The Iran-backed group in Yemen has threatened to target Saudi vessels in a vital shipping route.

items.headline: Watch: Driver dressed as Spider-Man whisks man in a wheelchair across busy road
items.summary: Traffic junction footage captured the moment a man dressed as Spider-Man helped a man cross a busy highway .

items.headline: CJP protest: The youth movement India's Modi didn't see coming
items.summary: The CJP protest began with exam paper leaks. It became India's biggest student movement in years - why?

items.headline: Indonesian babies were trafficked to Singapore. What happens to them now?
items.summary: At least 12 babies have been trafficked from Indonesia to Singapore, but what will happen to them remains unclear.

items.headline: Could you run a country on just three hours of sleep? Japan's PM says she does
items.summary: Japan’s prime minister Sanae Takaichi says she survives on zero to three hours a night, and people are reacting.

items.headline: Google fined €890m by EU for favouring its own apps over rivals
items.summary: It is the first major enforcement action against the tech giant under the EU's new digital rules.

items.headline: Lawmakers push for AI 'kill switch' after OpenAI models go rogue
items.summary: A new bill would let the US government order the shutdown of AI models that pose a public threat.

items.headline: Google and Tesla shares plunge as AI spending rattles markets
items.summary: As major tech companies race to capitalize on a new wave of AI technology, investors wonder when financial benefits will appear.

items.headline: Amazon gaming boss predicts future where players no longer need consoles
items.summary: Jeff Gattis said the rising cost of gaming tech is likely to mean more people will stream their games online.

items.headline: 'Let's enjoy the ride' says Elon Musk, as AI fears mount
items.summary: Talking to The Economist, Musk said he doesn't care if people hate him and said he was not a racist.

items.headline: Teenager drops social media addiction lawsuit against Meta
items.summary: Claims from a 15-year-old boy were set to go to trial next week in Los Angeles, but the case has now been dropped.

items.headline: OpenAI says its AI went rogue and launched 'unprecedented' cyber-attack
items.summary: It is one of the first publicly disclosed cyber-attacks carried out by AI without direct human involvement.

items.headline: Tech Life
items.summary: Dogs have a great sense of smell. Now machines are being trained to sniff out odours.

items.headline: Microsoft responds to LG monitors installing McAfee ads on Windows
items.summary: App is installed through Windows Update when certain LG monitors connect to a PC.

items.headline: FDA reports new outbreak of explosive diarrhea with 72 cases identified
items.summary: The FDA hasn't said where the cases are or how they're linked.

items.headline: Forgot your Google password? Now you can log in with a selfie.
items.summary: Google's selfie videos can be used for account access, AI Avatars, and age verification.

items.headline: AI Kill Switch Act would let Trump admin order shutdown of rogue AI systems
items.summary: Bill would let Homeland Security chief decide when an AI should be shut down.

items.headline: Final Coyote vs. Acme trailer drops at SDCC
items.summary: Also, the full trailer for Zach Cregger's Resident Evil features a likable everyman.

items.headline: A woman got a UTI. Two years later, the bacteria had evolved, invaded her brain.
items.summary: The case provides a novel report of the emergence of heterovirulence.

items.headline: Google hit with $1 billion in fines as EU braces for Trump battle
items.summary: Google becomes third tech giant to face huge fines under the Digital Markets Act.

items.headline: Sony's decision to ditch discs was practically inevitable, data shows
items.summary: Circana numbers show US gamers overwhelmingly voting with their wallets.

items.headline: How AI guardrails are impeding the work of offensive cybersecurity researchers
items.summary: We spoke with several cybersecurity researchers, who look for unknown vulnerabilities and develop tools to exploit them, about how OpenAI’s and Anthropic’s guardrails affect their work.

items.headline: AMD takes on Nvidia with its Helios AI rack-scale system
items.summary: AMD is challenging its chipmaker rival with a new rack-scale system that will start shipping to customers later this year.

items.headline: Anthropic updates Claude voice mode with more capable models
items.summary: Claude's new voice model will let you reschedule your meeting or draft an email.

items.headline: AegisAI, founded by former Google security execs, lands $36M to stop AI-driven spear phishing
items.summary: AegisAI co-founders developed AI agents that quickly analyze each message as a human would, paying attention to small anomalies that even the most elaborate checklist wouldn’t catch.

items.headline: Runway launches AI model router as generative media gets crowded
items.summary: The Media Router is a tool that automatically selects the best image, video, or audio generation model for a request based on whether a developer prioritizes quality, speed or cost.

items.headline: OpenAI makes ChatGPT Health available to all US users
items.summary: Users can also integrate their personal data from services like Apple Health, Function, and MyFitnessPal.

items.headline: Meta launched a new AI optimism ad set to a song about human extinction
items.summary: David Bowie's song "Five Years," which Meta used in a supposedly inspiring advertisement, is about humans learning that they have five years left to live before the apocalypse.

items.headline: Nvidia is sending GPUs to the moon
items.summary: If there's a place in the universe without GPUs, Nvidia is sending them there.

items.headline: Alexa Plus is getting an AI update to handle more complicated instructions
items.summary: Amazon is launching an update to its Alexa Plus assistant that will allow it to connect to smart home devices in new ways. With the update, which is currently in preview, Alexa Plus can link up with tech from Bosch, Delta, Ecovacs, iRobot, Yale Home, Whirlpool, Tapo, Eufy, and ot

items.headline: Claude’s voice mode is now available for Opus and Sonnet
items.summary: Until now, voice mode has only been available on Claude Haiku, Anthropic's faster but less powerful model. Now the company is making its Opus and Sonnet models available in voice mode, and extending its reach into apps like Gmail, Slack, and Canva. When Anthropic launched voice m

items.headline: Patreon is laying off 20 percent of workers
items.summary: Patreon is laying off 20 percent of its workers, or around 93 employees, as reported earlier by 404 Media. In a memo to employees, Patreon CEO Jack Conte writes that the company isn't making these changes "because we believe AI replaces humans," but says AI has "fundamentally tra

items.headline: OpenAI is making big claims as it rolls out ChatGPT Health to everyone
items.summary: OpenAI is rolling out ChatGPT Health to everyone in the US on Thursday, allowing more people to connect their medical records and health-tracking information to the chatbot. During a briefing, Ashley Alexander, OpenAI's vice president of health product, says the company's models

items.headline: Lawmakers prepare bill requiring AI ‘kill switch’
items.summary: Lawmakers are preparing to introduce an "AI Kill Switch Act" that would require AI companies to shut down or throttle their systems on orders from the Department of Homeland Security, according to a report from Politico. Reps. Ted Lieu (D-CA) and Nathaniel Moran (R-TX) are expect

items.headline: Apple’s OpenAI lawsuit is about who gets to define the post-smartphone era
items.summary: Today on Decoder, I’m talking with Hayden Field, The Verge’s senior AI reporter, about the major trade secrets lawsuit between Apple and OpenAI and what this tells us about OpenAI’s future. By now I’m sure most Decoder listeners are familiar with Apple’s allegations in this case.

items.headline: The right-wing boomers protesting data centers have a lot in common with the left
items.summary: A person holds signs during a nationwide protest against AI data center expansion outside Peace Hall in New Port Richey, Florida. | AFP via Getty Images On a gray, humid Saturday morning in central Florida, a little under a dozen people gathered outside the Spring Hill Branch Lib

items.headline: Here’s what Samsung’s smart glasses actually look like
items.summary: With a camera on every pair, Google’s and Samsung’s AI glasses face the same privacy problems as Meta’s. | Photo: Dominic Preston / The Verge Samsung has given us our first chance to check out its upcoming smart glasses in person, revealing two new designs and the first specs in

items.headline: Google just redesigned the search box for the first time in 25 years — here’s why it matters more than you think.
items.summary: For a quarter century, the Google search box has been one of the most recognizable interfaces in computing: a thin white rectangle, a blinking cursor, a few typed words, and a list of blue links. On Tuesday, Google will formally retire that paradigm. At its annual I/O developer c

items.headline: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
items.summary: Railway, a San Francisco-based cloud platform that has quietly amassed two million developers without spending a dollar on marketing, announced Thursday that it raised $100 million in a Series B funding round, as surging demand for artificial intelligence applications exposes the

items.headline: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
items.summary: The artificial intelligence coding revolution comes with a catch: it's expensive. Claude Code, Anthropic's terminal-based AI agent that can write, debug, and deploy code autonomously, has captured the imagination of software developers worldwide. But its pricing — ranging from $2

items.headline: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
items.summary: Alfred Wahlforss was running out of options. His startup, Listen Labs, needed to hire over 100 engineers, but competing against Mark Zuckerberg's $100 million offers seemed impossible. So he spent $5,000 — a fifth of his marketing budget — on a billboard in San Francisco displayi

items.headline: Salesforce rolls out new Slackbot AI agent as it battles Microsoft and Google in workplace AI
items.summary: Salesforce on Tuesday launched an entirely rebuilt version of Slackbot, the company's workplace assistant, transforming it from a simple notification tool into what executives describe as a fully powered AI agent capable of searching enterprise data, drafting documents, and takin

items.headline: Anthropic launches Cowork, a Claude Desktop agent that works in your files — no coding required
items.summary: Anthropic released Cowork on Monday, a new AI agent capability that extends the power of its wildly successful Claude Code tool to non-technical users — and according to company insiders, the team built the entire feature in approximately a week and a half, largely using Claude C

items.headline: Nous Research's NousCoder-14B is an open-source coding model landing right in the Claude Code moment
items.summary: Nous Research, the open-source artificial intelligence startup backed by crypto venture firm Paradigm, released a new competitive programming model on Monday that it says matches or exceeds several larger proprietary systems — trained in just four days using 48 of Nvidia's latest

items.headline: How AI helps scientists design the next generation of medicines
items.summary: Designing and developing a new medicine is an expensive, failure-prone scientific challenge. A new drug can take many years to develop, at the cost of a significant investment. And even then, most possible candidates never reach the patient. For biologic medicines, therapies made

items.headline: Advancing next-gen AI with materials science innovation
items.summary: The conversation about AI often centers on algorithms, computing power, or huge investments in new semiconductor fabrication plants and hyperscale data centers. But beneath each of these advances is another layer of innovation that makes them possible: advanced materials. Every n

items.headline: China’s AI models have Trump’s AI world at war with itself
items.summary: This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here. Over the weekend, several current and former advisors to President Donald Trump on AI publicly lobbed insults at the country’s leading AI comp

items.headline: AI is more likely than humans to form biases when hiring
items.summary: The next time you apply for a job, AI may screen your résumé before any human sees it. But there’s good reason to question whether AI will judge you fairly. Researchers already know that LLMs pick up human biases from their training data. New research suggests that LLMs can also

items.headline: The risk of weather data sabotage is rising
items.summary: Every morning, airline dispatchers, grid operators, and farmers around the world make decisions based on the same thing: a weather forecast. While these forecasts are something that most people glance at for two seconds, weather predictions influence major strategic decisions in

items.headline: Meet GPT-Red: an LLM super-hacker OpenAI built to make its models safer
items.summary: OpenAI has built an LLM super-hacker called GPT-Red that it uses as a sparring partner to help its other models boost their defenses against cyberattacks. Last week the company released the latest version of its flagship LLM, GPT-5.6. OpenAI says that training it against GPT-Red

items.headline: What Anthropic’s latest AI discovery does—and doesn’t—show
items.summary: This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here. Anthropic—currently the world’s most valuable AI company, with a nearly $1 trillion valuation—has a reputation for publishing strange and head

items.headline: Anthropic found a hidden space where Claude puzzles over concepts
items.summary: The AI firm Anthropic has developed a technique that has given it the clearest glimpse yet at what’s really going on inside large language models as they answer questions or carry out tasks. What they found ranges from the mundane to the unnerving. Researchers at the company buil

items.headline: Meta’s New Feel-Good AI Ad Uses a Song About the World Ending
items.summary: The clip features the David Bowie track “Five Years,” which includes lyrics such as “Earth was really dying (dying).”

items.headline: Remember Jibo? Its Successor Is a Wearable That Turns Your Life Into AI Slop
items.summary: With “blessings” from the original Jibo founders, iKairos is a wearable or desk-mounted “AI journal” that turns your family moments into AI images and video.

items.headline: The White House Is Trying to Figure Out What to Do About Chinese AI
items.summary: There’s a debate going on in the Trump administration over how to handle increasingly powerful Chinese AI models.

items.headline: China’s Open AI Models Are Challenging Silicon Valley’s Playbook
items.summary: As access to Anthropic’s and OpenAI’s frontier models becomes more restricted, Chinese labs are pitching their open-source alternatives as stable, accessible, and increasingly capable.

items.headline: OpenAI Models Escaped Containment and Hacked Hugging Face
items.summary: The cybersecurity-focused models, including GPT-5.6 Sol, broke out of a testing sandbox, exploited a zero-day, and gained access to the open internet to pull off the attack.

items.headline: This Former Intel CEO Wants to Jumpstart Moore’s Law With Light
items.summary: Pat Gelsinger wants to pave the way to ever more powerful artificial intelligence using tiny beams of light.

items.headline: A Sneaky Hacking Tool Targeting AI Infrastructure Is Lurking in Victims’ Blind Spots
items.summary: A new type of malware can worm deep into AI coding systems to steal data and logins—and can flip a “death switch” to destroy files and keep out real users.

items.headline: Nvidia Wants to Own Every Chip Inside AI Data Centers
items.summary: Nvidia’s Vera Rubin platform combines CPUs and GPUs into a single system, reflecting the company’s growing ambition to power every layer of AI infrastructure.
